title: balsamorhiza incana
intro: Balsamorhiza incana, also known as the canyon balsamroot, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). Native to the western United States and Canada, it has been used by indigenous peoples for centuries for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems are rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Balsamorhiza incana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health practitioners.
